label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking what the dance project aims to do. The options are A, B, C, D. First, I need to go back to the passage and find the main purpose of the project. Let me read through the text again. LaMar Baylor teaches dance to street boys in Rwanda as part of the Rebecca Davis Dance Company. The project helps them learn dance and how to behave in a classroom environment. Rebecca Davis started the project after visiting Rwanda in 2008 and seeing street children dancing. She thought dance could get them off the streets into a safe place. She believes learning dance is a step toward education. After learning to attend classes and follow directions, they can take IT classes. The best students get scholarships to a boarding school. So the project's aim isn't just dance; it's part of a broader educational path. The key points are providing structured learning, self-expression, and eventually leading them to education (like IT classes) and scholarships. Looking at the options: A) Parental care - The passage says the children have lost their families, but the project doesn't mention providing parental figures, just dance and education. B) Provide scholarships - While scholarships are mentioned, they are a result of performing well in the classes, not the main aim of the project. The project's goal is broader than just scholarships. C) Help them receive some education - The text says that dance is a step toward education. Learning to attend classes, follow directions, then moving on to IT classes. So education is a key part of the project's aim. D) Keep them healthy
